seoTitle: Create a Custom Legend for Power BI Map Visuals
metaDescription: Learn how to build a custom legend for map visuals in Power BI so viewers can read regional data at a glance instead of guessing at colors.
intro: A map without a clear legend forces viewers to guess what the colors mean, which undermines an otherwise strong visual. This guide covers how to create a custom legend for map visuals in Power BI so your geographic data reads clearly the first time. It's aimed at report builders who want maps that actually communicate, not just decorate.

steps.heading: Understand why a custom legend matters
steps.body: A legend gives viewers the key to interpreting colors or sizes on your map, which is essential when comparing regions like sales territories or performance zones.

steps.heading: Build the legend step by step
steps.body: Configure the legend settings on your map visual, defining categories or value ranges so each color or marker has a clear, labeled meaning.

steps.heading: Improve clarity for your audience
steps.body: Adjust label text, positioning, and formatting so the legend is easy to read without crowding the map itself.

steps.heading: Apply it to a business use case
steps.body: Use the finished legend in a real report, such as showing regional sales performance, so stakeholders can interpret the map without extra explanation.

steps.heading: Consider automation for updates
steps.body: Set the legend to reflect underlying data changes automatically, so it stays accurate as new regions or values are added.

faq.question: Why doesn't Power BI's default map legend look clear enough?
faq.answer: The default legend often uses generic labels or colors that don't match your specific business categories, which is why a custom legend improves readability.

faq.question: Can I customize legend colors to match my brand?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust the color scheme used in the legend and map to align with your organization's branding or reporting standards.

faq.question: Does a custom legend work with all Power BI map types?
faq.answer: Custom legends work with most standard and filled map visuals, though the exact configuration options can vary slightly by visual type.

faq.question: Will the legend update automatically if my data changes?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the legend is tied to the underlying fields rather than hardcoded values, it updates automatically when the data refreshes.
